target: SEC-6.13 (Part 6.13 The MOND Grouping) — close the third-option dome-defender move: galaxies as 2D illusory firmament-projected patterns whose photon measurements happen to fall along RAR/El-Gordo/Cepheid shapes. Per curmudgeon SEC-6.13-mond-grouping.json hole h3 and tracker entry EXP-426 (routed from ISS-2113).
insertion_point:
After the existing "two options" paragraph (the last paragraph of the h3 "Why the closed-world commitment forecloses the derivation route" subsection), immediately before

The category placement, and the self-own

.
current_text_html: The dome model has, in published form, two options for what galaxies are. Either they are real distant gravitating mass-collections — in which case the firmament is not bounded, and the dome model is something other than the dome model — or they are firmament artifacts at sub-30,000-kilometer distances — in which case mainstream measurements of galactic phenomena cannot be marshaled as evidence for the dome, because the measurements were of phenomena the dome cosmology denies exist. The MOND-grouping WINs implicitly take the first option, citing galactic anomalies as real and measured. The dome's bounded-firmament commitments require the second option. The two cannot both be held — which is the structure of self-contradicted, not merely misleading. That is the basis on which WIN-047, WIN-048, WIN-052, WIN-054, and WIN-055 are reclassified to Self-Contradicted under sub-rationale "category-error import (MOND grouping)."
replacement_text_html:
The dome model has, in published form, two options for what galaxies are. Either they are real distant gravitating mass-collections — in which case the firmament is not bounded, and the dome model is something other than the dome model — or they are firmament artifacts at sub-30,000-kilometer distances — in which case mainstream measurements of galactic phenomena cannot be marshaled as evidence for the dome, because the measurements were of phenomena the dome cosmology denies exist. The MOND-grouping WINs implicitly take the first option, citing galactic anomalies as real and measured. The dome's bounded-firmament commitments require the second option. The two cannot both be held — which is the structure of self-contradicted, not merely misleading. That is the basis on which WIN-047, WIN-048, WIN-052, WIN-054, and WIN-055 are reclassified to Self-Contradicted under sub-rationale "category-error import (MOND grouping)."

A third reading the dome could attempt — galaxies as 2D illusory patterns on the firmament whose photon properties nevertheless fall along an RAR-shaped curve when measured — does not escape the contradiction. In that reading, the measured Radial Acceleration Relation is a property of the illusion-generation mechanism, not of any 3D mass distribution; the same applies to El Gordo's lensing-reconstructed cluster mass at z ≈ 0.87, the Cepheid period-luminosity ladder, and the CMB quadrupole-octupole alignment — each measurement probes whatever rules the firmament-projection runs by, not the galactic-scale 3D physics that MOND, NFW mass profiles, and standard cosmology actually invoke. The per-WIN treatment states this dilemma directly (data/wins.json:WIN-052 detail_extra): option (A) the objects exist at the claimed distances, in which case the firmament is falsified by direct observation; option (B) the objects are illusions whose measured properties cannot serve as evidence for the dome. The third-option retreat does not produce an option (C); it produces a more sophisticated version of option (B) — the dome still cannot cite the measurements as confirmations of a cosmology whose explanatory mechanism (galactic-scale 3D physics) it has now denied operates.
rationale_summary: Curmudgeon SEC-6.13-mond-grouping.json hole h3 (moderate) flagged that the "two options" framing — galaxies real (firmament unbounded) or galaxies firmament-artifacts (measurements invalid) — does not explicitly close a third move a dome defender could attempt: galaxies as 2D illusory patterns on the firmament that nevertheless produce real photon spectra mapping onto an RAR-shaped curve, El-Gordo-shaped lensing signal, etc. A sympathetic defender could argue "the data is real, the dome is just agnostic about what produces the data." The closure: that reading collapses the measurements to probes of the illusion-generation mechanism, not of 3D extragalactic mass distributions; since the MOND-community framings explicitly invoke galactic-scale 3D physics, the illusion reading concedes by construction that those framings do not apply, so the imported anomalies cannot count as confirmations of a cosmology in which galactic-scale physics is the explanatory mechanism. The third-option retreat produces a more sophisticated option (B), not an option (C) — same conclusion, slightly tighter wording. Per-WIN treatment in data/wins.json:WIN-052 detail_extra already states the (A)/(B) dilemma directly; the section-level addition now cross-references it.
holes_addressed: SEC-6.13.h3 (moderate): "The two-options framing on galaxies — either real (firmament unbounded) or firmament artifacts (measurements invalid) — does not explicitly close a third dome-defender move: galaxies as 2D illusory patterns on the firmament that nonetheless yield real measurement results when probed." Now explicitly closed via the new paragraph, which folds the 2D-illusion retreat into a sharpened option (B).
new_evidence_added: Cross-reference to data/wins.json:WIN-052 detail_extra — the inescapable-dilemma framing (option A: objects exist at claimed distances; option B: objects are illusions, measurements cannot serve as evidence). The per-WIN treatment has carried this argument since the WIN-052 SC reclassification; the section-level prose now points at it.

Explicit list of the four MOND-grouping observables affected by the third-option retreat: RAR, El Gordo lensing mass at z ≈ 0.87, Cepheid period-luminosity ladder, CMB quadrupole-octupole alignment. Each is named so a reader can verify that the illusion reading defangs each one in turn.

Three MOND-community framework names invoked by the closure (Milgromian dynamics, NFW mass profiles, standard cosmology) — these are the frameworks the imported WINs implicitly rely on for legitimacy, and they all assume galactic-scale 3D physics by construction.

anticipated_objections.objection: The illusion reading is a strawman — no dome defender has actually argued this. Closing it pre-emptively wastes prose budget on an unraised retreat.
anticipated_objections.response: The curmudgeon h3 hole flagged it specifically as the dome's likely tactical retreat once the (A)/(B) dilemma is published in the per-WIN reviews. The dome author has previously retreated to "the firmament is what we see, what lies beyond is unspecified" framings on adjacent topics (Glasswing CMB axis), so the precedent exists. Closing it in print, with one short paragraph, is cheap insurance against the move and makes the section-level argument as tight as the per-WIN reviews already are.

anticipated_objections.objection: The closure repeats what is already in WIN-052 detail_extra. Why duplicate it at the section level?
anticipated_objections.response: The detail_extra dilemma is per-WIN; a reader who lands on Part 6.13 may not have read WIN-052's detail panel. The section-level paragraph cross-references the per-WIN treatment (data/wins.json:WIN-052 detail_extra) rather than reproducing the framing verbatim, and adds the section-specific contribution: applying the (A)/(B) dilemma to all four MOND-grouping observables in one shot (RAR, El Gordo, Cepheid ladder, CMB axis) and explicitly tagging the MOND-community frameworks that the illusion reading by construction denies.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new paragraph adds ~170 words to an already-dense subsection. Part 6.13 was already at 13.8 KB; this pushes toward 15 KB.
anticipated_objections.response: Part 6 subsections span 3.3 KB to 22.8 KB; 15 KB is mid-band, not outlier. The new paragraph carries the closure for a moderate-severity hole (h3) and a cross-reference that strengthens the section's evidential chain into the per-WIN reviews. The TLDR is unchanged, so the at-a-glance reader is unaffected. The denser body is the right tradeoff for the deep-attack class — readers who open the details want the closure.

anticipated_objections.objection: The closure says "galactic-scale 3D physics" as if that is the only explanatory mechanism the imported WINs invoke. What if a defender argues the WINs invoke "effective" descriptions agnostic to 3D-ness?
anticipated_objections.response: MOND itself is defined by a critical acceleration a₀ ≈ 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰ m/s² that becomes relevant at 3D distances of order 10²⁰ m between observer and dominant gravitating mass. There is no "effective" MOND that doesn't reference 3D distances and mass distributions — Milgrom's original formulation, the McGaugh RAR fit, and the Banik/Kroupa El Gordo treatment all parameterize their predictions on 3D enclosed-mass profiles. A defender who claims "effective and dimensionless" framings would have to invent a non-MOND formalism that produces the same RAR curve without 3D distances; no such formalism is in the literature, and the dome author has not derived one.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author has a legitimate kernel here: photon spectra reaching ground-based observatories ARE real measurements regardless of whether the source is at 8 kpc or 8,000 km. A determined dome defender can correctly say "the RAR curve we measure is a real curve, you cannot deny the measurement." That part is correct. The closure does not deny the measurement; it concedes the measurement and shifts the question to interpretation: under the dome's own bounded-firmament parameters, the only interpretation available is "the measurement probes whatever rules the firmament-projection runs by" — which is not a refutation of the dome's internal consistency, but it IS a refutation of the WIN-level claim that this measurement confirms disc cosmology. The dome can retreat to "we are not predicting the physics, we are predicting the data signature," but at that point the WIN category ("confirmed prediction") is the wrong category — the dome's own ΛCDM Counter-Prediction sub-category (visible on WIN-048) is.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel concedes the data, not the cosmological interpretation. The five reclassified WINs (047, 048, 052, 054, 055) are filed as confirmed WINs because the registry treats them as evidence FOR disc cosmology. Once the third-option retreat is on the table, "evidence for disc cosmology" becomes "evidence for whatever rules the firmament-projection follows when generating these specific data signatures." That is not nothing — but it is not what a "confirmed WIN" claims. The author's own ΛCDM Counter-Prediction sub-category was the right category for this kind of material. The third-option closure does not refute the data; it forces the placement choice ("WIN" vs "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction") back into view, which is exactly where the self-own already lives.
summary_for_decider:
Targeted paragraph insertion in SEC-6.13 (p6-613-mond-grouping subsection of part6.html), immediately after the existing "two options" paragraph and before the next

The category placement, and the self-own

. Adds one ~187-word paragraph closing the dome's third-option defender retreat (galaxies as 2D illusory firmament patterns whose photon properties produce RAR-shaped curves) by folding it into a sharpened option (B), enumerating the four MOND-grouping observables that the retreat affects (RAR / El Gordo / Cepheid ladder / CMB axis), naming the MOND-community frameworks (Milgromian dynamics, NFW profiles, standard cosmology) the retreat by construction denies, and cross-referencing per-WIN treatment at data/wins.json:WIN-052 detail_extra. Closes ISS-2113 (SEC-6.13.h3 moderate). Word count: 140 → 327 (+187). Build impact: rebuild required. Test impact: none — no DATA blob / layer count / classifier / numeric rate touched; pure prose addition. TLDR unchanged. Declared review_class deep-attack per tracker entry (ISS-2113 class_hint=deep-attack) — the addition introduces a new argument (the third-option closure + per-WIN cross-reference) rather than refining an existing one, so curmudgeon-on-rewrite needs fresh attack work.
